mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 2.0% and continues climbing, the cost of capital increases, reducing the present value of future corporate earnings. This monetary tightening regime produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as equity valuations compress.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 2.0% and rising, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power. A month-over-month increase in UNRATE of more than 0.2 percentage points predicts negative equity returns over the next 5 trading days as forward earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between UNRATE monthly changes and 5-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP (GDPC1) above trend signals robust economic expansion and rising corporate profitability. When GDPC1 grows quarter-over-quarter at rates exceeding 2.0% annualized, S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days are positive as earnings growth accelerates.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19699.465 billion (2017 dollars) as of 2022-04-01
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during periods of GDPC1 growth > 2.0% annualized are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and employment (PAYEMS) jointly signal supply-side capacity utilization. When both INDPRO and PAYEMS grow month-over-month simultaneously, the economy is operating at higher capacity with improving productivity, driving positive 20-day forward returns through margin expansion.
candidate_trigger: both INDPRO and PAYEMS increase month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint positive months of INDPRO and PAYEMS do not produce significantly higher 20-day forward returns than baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 3.0% year-over-year forces the Federal Reserve into sustained tightening, which compresses equity valuations. When CPI inflation exceeds 3.0%, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative as real discount rates rise and growth expectations fall.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during high-inflation periods (CPI YoY > 3.0%) are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window in inflationary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly capture consumer demand momentum. When both HOUST and RSAFS decline month-over-month, consumer confidence and discretionary spending are weakening, predicting negative 20-day forward returns as forward earnings guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: both HOUST and RSAFS decline month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1446.0 thousand units as of 2022-07-01
fact:RSAFS at 683291.0 million dollars as of 2022-08-01
failure_condition: Joint negative months of HOUST and RSAFS do not produce significantly lower 20-day forward returns than baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth is a leading indicator of sustained economic activity. When PAYEMS grows month-over-month by more than 0.1%, labor market momentum is positive, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following PAYEMS growth > 0.1% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises signals stagflationary pressure: supply constraints combined with weakening demand. This regime produces negative 60-day forward returns as profit margins compress and growth expectations fall simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month AND UNRATE rises month-over-month
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during simultaneous INDPRO decline and UNRATE rise are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).
